projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: Cartesian Coordinate Quest — Reflections & Distance
projects.desc: Reflect points across the x- and y-axes and read the new coordinates (6.NS.6b).

projects.title: Archipelago Coordinate Navigator
projects.desc: Plot and reflect ordered pairs across all four quadrants (6.NS.6b).

title: Reflect Points Across Axes
contentObjective: I can reflect points across the x-axis and y-axis on the coordinate plane.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words reflection, x-axis, y-axis, and symmetry.

turnAndTalk.question: When you reflect the point (3, 2) over the y-axis, which coordinate changes its sign and which stays the same? Why?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Reflecting over the y-axis changes the ___ coordinate because ___.
turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student states reflecting over the y-axis flips the sign of the x-coordinate while the y-coordinate stays the same, so (3,2) becomes (-3,2).
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain why the reflected point is the same distance from the y-axis as the original.
extendStems: The reflected point is the same distance from the axis because ___.
A reflection is like a ___ over the line.

turnAndTalk.question: Reflect (4, -5) over the x-axis. Talk through how the coordinates change and where the new point lands.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Reflecting over the x-axis changes the ___ coordinate, so the point becomes ___.

stems.en: Over the x-axis, the ___ coordinate flips sign.
stems.es: Sobre el eje x, la coordenada ___ cambia de signo.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student flips the sign of the y-coordinate, so (4, -5) becomes (4, 5), and explains the x stays the same.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to predict where (4, -5) lands if reflected over the y-axis instead, and compare the two results.
extendStems: The two reflections are different because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How is reflecting a point over an axis like looking at it in a mirror placed on that axis?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A reflection is like a mirror because ___.

stems.en: The image is the same distance away but on the ___ side.
stems.es: La imagen esta a la misma distancia pero del ___ lado.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student connects the axis to a mirror line and the reflected point to a mirror image, equal distance on the opposite side.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to generalize a rule: what changes when you reflect over the x-axis versus the y-axis?
extendStems: Reflecting over the x-axis changes ___, while reflecting over the y-axis changes ___.
A rule I can always use is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A classmate reflected (2, 3) over the x-axis and got (-2, 3). What did they do wrong, and what is the correct image?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The mistake was changing the ___ coordinate, but the correct image is ___.

stems.en: Over the x-axis, they should have changed the ___ coordinate.
stems.es: Sobre el eje x, debieron cambiar la coordenada ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student identifies they flipped x instead of y; the correct image over the x-axis is (2, -3).
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique how to remember which coordinate changes for each axis.
extendStems: To remember which coordinate changes, I think ___.
Reflecting over the x-axis affects the ___ value because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Reflect Points Across Axes, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

stems.en: Over the x-axis, the ___ coordinate flips sign.
stems.es: Sobre el eje x, la coordenada ___ cambia de signo.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.NS.6b — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A flipped image of a point on the other side of a line, the same distance away.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una imagen volteada de un punto al otro lado de una línea, a la misma distancia.
vocabulary.visual: (3, 2) reflected over the y-axis becomes (-3, 2) — x changes sign, y stays
vocabulary.cloze: A flip of a point or shape over an axis to make a mirror image is a ___.

examples.why: It is a mirror image across the axis.
That is a slide, not a reflection.

vocabulary.definitionVi: Phép lật hình hoặc điểm qua một đường thẳng (trục đối xứng).
vocabulary.definitionAr: قلب شكل أو نقطة فوق خط مستقيم (محور الانعكاس).

vocabulary.definition: The line that goes across the grid. Flipping over it changes the y sign.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La línea que va de lado en la cuadrícula. Voltear sobre ella cambia el signo de y.
vocabulary.visual: (4, 3) → (4, -3): the y flips from +3 to -3, like folding the paper along the horizontal line
vocabulary.cloze: The horizontal number line on the coordinate plane is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Trục nằm ngang trên mặt phẳng tọa độ.

vocabulary.definition: The line that goes up the grid. Flipping over it changes the x sign.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La línea que va hacia arriba en la cuadrícula. Voltear sobre ella cambia el signo de x.
vocabulary.visual: (4, 3) → (-4, 3): the x flips from +4 to -4, like folding the paper along the vertical line
vocabulary.cloze: The vertical number line on the coordinate plane is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Trục thẳng đứng trên mặt phẳng tọa độ.

vocabulary.definition: When a shape looks the same on both sides of a line.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cuando una figura se ve igual a ambos lados de una línea.
vocabulary.visual: A butterfly's wings are symmetric — fold it down the middle and both sides match
vocabulary.cloze: When two halves are mirror images of each other, the figure has ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Đặc tính của hình dạng cân đối và đối xứng nhau qua tâm hoặc trục.
vocabulary.definitionAr: تطابق نصفي الشكل عند طيه أو لفه.

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, like -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números enteros y sus opuestos, como -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite, with no fraction part, is an ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Tập hợp các số nguyên dương, nguyên âm và số không.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مجموعة الأعداد الكاملة الموجبة والسالبة والصفر.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega discovers that her treasure map has a magic mirror trick! When she folds the map along the x-axis, some clues match up perfectly with clues on the other side. For example, a marker at (2, 4) has a matching marker at (2, -4) when reflected over the x-axis. Another pair reflects over the y-axis: (5, 3) and (-5, 3). How does reflection change the coordinates?
launch.contextImage: Treasure map folded along an axis showing mirrored treasure markers on both sides
noticePrompts: When (2, 4) reflects over the x-axis to (2, -4), which coordinate changed?
When (5, 3) reflects over the y-axis to (-5, 3), which coordinate changed?
What stays the same in each reflection?
wonderPrompts: What happens if you reflect a point over BOTH axes?
Can a point be its own reflection?

conceptIntro.heading: How do you reflect a point across an axis?
conceptIntro.intro: A reflection is a flip of a point over a line, like a mirror image. The axis you flip over decides which coordinate changes its sign.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I will reflect (3, 2) over the y-axis.
The y-axis is the vertical line, so the point flips left or right. That changes the x-coordinate.
The x changes sign: 3 becomes -3. The y stays the same: 2 stays 2.
So the reflection of (3, 2) over the y-axis is (-3, 2).

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now let's reflect (4, -5) over the x-axis. Which coordinate changes over the x-axis? The y-coordinate.
What does -5 become when it changes sign? It becomes 5.
The x stays 4, so the reflection is what point? It is (4, 5).

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the explore, plot each point like (3, 5) and (-4, 2), then plot its reflection across the given axis.

explore.instructions: Plot each original point and then plot its reflection across the given axis.
explore.label: Plot each point and its reflection. Verify the reflection rule.

discourse.prompt: What pattern do you notice about the coordinates when reflecting over the x-axis versus the y-axis?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When I reflect over the x-axis, the ___ -coordinate changes sign and the ___ -coordinate stays the same. When I reflect over the y-axis, the ___ -coordinate changes sign and the ___ -coordinate stays the same.

optional.stem: Captain Vega folds the map along the x-axis. A marker at (7, 2) lands on its mirror image. What are the new coordinates?
optional.explanation: Folding along (reflecting across) the x-axis flips the sign of the y-coordinate only. (7, 2) becomes (7, -2).

optional.stem: A buried chest is marked at (-5, 3). Vega folds the map along the y-axis to find its mirror twin. Where is the twin?
optional.explanation: Reflecting across the y-axis flips the sign of the x-coordinate only. (-5, 3) becomes (5, 3).

optional.stem: Vega reflects a palm-tree marker at (8, -6) across the x-axis. Which coordinate changes its sign?
optional.explanation: Across the x-axis the rule is (x, y) -> (x, -y), so only the y-coordinate flips sign. (8, -6) becomes (8, 6).

optional.label: Match each map marker and fold line to its mirror image.

optional.instructions: Sort each fold by which coordinate rule it follows on Vega's map.

categories.label: Over x-axis: keep x, flip y (x, -y)
Over y-axis: flip x, keep y (-x, y)

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Vega's Fold Mix-Up

workedExample.label: Problem
workedExample.work: Reflect the marker (6, 4) across the x-axis.

workedExample.label: Step 1
workedExample.work: The x-axis is the fold line, so flip the sign of the x-coordinate.

workedExample.label: Step 2
workedExample.work: Change 6 to -6 and keep y as 4.

optional.correctWork: Reflecting across the x-axis follows the rule (x, y) -> (x, -y): keep the x-coordinate and flip the y-coordinate. Vega flipped the x instead of the y. The correct mirror image of (6, 4) is (6, -4).
hints: The axis you fold over is the one that stays put. Folding over the x-axis means the x-value should NOT change.
Across the x-axis: keep x, flip y -> (x, -y). So (6, 4) should become (6, -4), not (-6, 4).

approaching.stem: What is the reflection of (4, -3) over the x-axis?
approaching.explanation: Reflecting over the x-axis changes the sign of the y-coordinate. The x stays the same (4), and the y changes from -3 to 3. The answer is (4, 3).

approaching.stem: What is the reflection of (-2, 6) over the y-axis?
approaching.explanation: Reflecting over the y-axis changes the sign of the x-coordinate. x goes from -2 to 2, and y stays 6. The answer is (2, 6).

approaching.stem: Which point is on the y-axis?
approaching.explanation: Points on the y-axis have x-coordinate = 0. (0, 5) is on the y-axis.

approaching.label: Match each point and axis to its reflection.
Put the steps in order for reflecting (5, -3) over the y-axis.
items: Identify the axis of reflection: y-axis
The y-axis is vertical, so the x-coordinate changes sign
Change x from 5 to -5, keep y as -3
correctOrder: Identify the axis of reflection: y-axis
The y-axis is vertical, so the x-coordinate changes sign
Change x from 5 to -5, keep y as -3

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Reflect Points Across Axes.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

onLevel.label: Plot (1, 4) and its reflections: over the x-axis, over the y-axis, and over both axes

onLevel.instructions: Find the reflection of each point over the given axis.

pairs.match: Reflects to itself over any axis

onLevel.label: Match each reflection rule to its description.

onLevel.stem: Point A is at (-3, 5). It is reflected over the x-axis to A', then A' is reflected over the y-axis to A''. What are the coordinates of A''?
onLevel.explanation: Over x-axis: (-3, 5) → (-3, -5). Then over y-axis: (-3, -5) → (3, -5). Both coordinates changed sign.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Reflect Points Across Axes.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign.

extending.title: Find the Reflection Error

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: The y-axis is vertical, so I change the y-coordinate

extending.correctWork: Reflecting over the y-axis changes the x-coordinate, not the y-coordinate. The correct reflection of (-3, 5) over the y-axis is (3, 5). The student confused which coordinate changes for each axis.
hints: The y-AXIS is the vertical line. Points flip ACROSS it, meaning they move left/right. Which coordinate controls left/right?
Over the y-axis: change x. Over the x-axis: change y. The name of the axis tells you which coordinate STAYS the same.

extending.prompt: A triangle has vertices at A(1, 2), B(4, 2), and C(4, 6). If you reflect the entire triangle over the y-axis, what are the new vertices? What do you notice about the size and shape of the reflected triangle compared to the original?
extending.sentenceFrame: The reflected vertices are A'(___), B'(___), C'(___). I found each by ___. The reflected triangle is ___ compared to the original because reflections ___.

extending.label: Plot the point (-2, 3). Reflect it over the x-axis, then reflect that result over the y-axis. Where do you end up? Now start over and reflect (-2, 3) over the y-axis first, then over the x-axis. Do you get the same final point?

optionalActivity.name: Mirror Map Reflection Lab
optionalActivity.intro: Fold Captain Vega's treasure map along an axis and find each marker's mirror image to unlock the next clue.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Reflect Points Across Axes is skipping the key idea: "Reflect over the x-axis: the y-coordinate changes sign. Reflect over the y-axis: the x-coordinate changes sign." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A game designer creates a spaceship at (3, 2) and wants to place enemy ships as reflections to make the level symmetric. One enemy is reflected over the y-axis, and another is reflected over the x-axis.
connect.promptQuestion: How do reflections help the game designer create a balanced, symmetric level?
connect.prompt: The enemy reflected over the y-axis is at ___ because ___. The enemy reflected over the x-axis is at ___ because ___. Reflections help game designers because ___.

exitTicket.stem: What is the reflection of (-5, 2) over the y-axis?
exitTicket.explanation: Reflecting over the y-axis changes the sign of the x-coordinate. x goes from -5 to 5, and y stays 2. The reflection is (5, 2).

printables.name: Reflection Mirror Map
printables.desc: Students reflect points across the axes and describe how the coordinate signs change.

printables.name: Reflect Points Across Axes — Color by Number
printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.
